serving.description: 1 serving (10 g), typical garnish or shredded accompaniment portion
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Bunga Kantan is approximately 10 g (about one tablespoon of finely shredded flower bud), used as an aromatic garnish or flavoring component in dishes such as Laksa, Rojak, or Asam Pedas.

nutrient_source.title: Etlingera elatior (Torch Ginger) — Nutritional Composition; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entry for edible ginger-family flowers and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables for Southeast Asian edible flowers
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central / FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Etlingera elatior flower bud. Values are adapted from FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables for edible tropical flowers and peer-reviewed phytochemical studies on Etlingera elatior, cross-checked against the input enriched JSON (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.9). The input values are broadly consistent with published literature ranges for this species. Vitamin D and Vitamin B12 are confirmed zero for a plant-based flower food. Saturated fat is estimated at 0.1 g per 100 g (typical for low-fat edible flowers); monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, and trans fat data are not available in approved sources for this specific food and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Edible Flowers and High-Fibre Tropical Plant Foods — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for high-fibre, low-carbohydrate tropical vegetables and edible plant parts
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Etlingera elatior flower bud exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 15 (low) based on the closest analogous high-fibre, low-carbohydrate tropical plant foods in the database (e.g., leafy vegetables and edible flowers with similar carbohydrate and fibre profiles). Glycemic load per 10 g serving = (15 × 0.85 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 0.13, rounded to 0.1. This is consistent with the food's very high dietary fibre content (4.5 g/100 g) and low available carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (8 mg/100 g) and high potassium (380 mg/100 g), supporting a favorable sodium-to-potassium ratio
hypertension.guidance: Bunga Kantan is well-suited for individuals with hypertension; its very low sodium content (8 mg/100 g) and high potassium (380 mg/100 g) support blood pressure regulation, and it can be used freely as a flavoring or garnish in sodium-restricted diets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (estimated ~15), low available carbohydrate (8.5 g/100 g), and high dietary fibre (4.5 g/100 g) attenuating postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Bunga Kantan is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; its low estimated glycemic index, high dietary fibre, and low caloric density (45 kcal/100 g) make it a suitable aromatic ingredient that contributes negligible glycemic load at typical serving sizes of 10 g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (0.5 g/100 g), negligible saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and high potassium with antioxidant phytochemicals
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Bunga Kantan poses minimal cardiovascular risk; its negligible fat and cholesterol content, combined with high potassium and antioxidant compounds documented in Etlingera elatior, make it a heart-friendly aromatic ingredient suitable for cardioprotective dietary patterns.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Rich in antioxidant phytochemicals with documented anti-inflammatory properties in Etlingera elatior
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Bunga Kantan may be beneficial for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its concentration of antioxidant and anti-inflammatory phytochemicals; it can be included freely as a flavoring ingredient in anti-inflammatory dietary approaches.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bunga Kantan, known in English as Torch Ginger Flower (Etlingera elatior), is a vibrant pink, torch-shaped flower bud that is one of the most distinctive aromatic ingredients in Malay, Peranakan, and Indonesian cuisines. The bud is prized for its complex floral, citrusy, and slightly peppery fragrance, which intensifies when finely shredded. It is a defining ingredient in Laksa, where it provides a refreshing high-pitched aroma that cuts through rich coconut or spicy broths. It is also used shredded raw in salads such as Rojak and Kerabu, and simmered in sour stews like Asam Pedas. In Indonesia it is known as Kecombrang or Honje, and in Thailand as Bongkot. Nutritionally, Bunga Kantan is low in calories (45 kcal/100 g), very low in fat (0.5 g/100 g), and provides moderate protein (2.2 g/100 g) and carbohydrate (8.5 g/100 g) with a notable dietary fibre content of 4.5 g/100 g. It is an excellent source of potassium (380 mg/100 g) and provides meaningful amounts of calcium, iron, vitamin A, and vitamin C. Its very low sodium content (8 mg/100 g) and high potassium make it suitable for hypertension management. The high fibre content and low estimated glycemic index (~15) make it appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Its negligible fat and cholesterol content support cardiovascular health. Bunga Kantan is naturally vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, and halal.

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source (USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or HPB Singapore) contains a dedicated entry for Etlingera elatior flower bud with full macro and micronutrient data. Values are adapted from FAO/INFOODS SEA edible tropical flower categories and cross-checked against peer-reviewed phytochemical literature on Etlingera elatior. The input enriched JSON (confidence 0.9, provenance ai_generated) is broadly consistent with published ranges. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg = 0.0) is confirmed zero as Bunga Kantan is a plant-based flower food that naturally contains no vitamin D. Vitamin B12 (vitamin_b12_mcg = 0.0) is confirmed zero as this is a plant food containing no vitamin B12. Cholesterol (cholesterol_mg = 0.0) is confirmed zero for this plant-based food. These zero values are naturally zero for a vegan plant food. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation and moisture data for Etlingera elatior flower bud are not available in USDA FoodData Central or FAO/INFOODS SEA approved sources as of 2026-04-16. 3. Saturated fat is estimated at 0.1 g/100 g based on typical values for low-fat edible tropical flowers in FAO/INFOODS SEA tables; no direct measurement found in approved sources. 4. GI is an analogous estimate (~15, low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for high-fibre, low-carbohydrate tropical plant foods; no direct GI measurement for Etlingera elatior exists in the database. 5. FoodEx2 code A00LC (edible flowers and flower buds) is used as the closest defensible parent category; no species-specific FoodEx2 code exists for Etlingera elatior. 6. FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03301710 is cited as the closest defensible match; reviewers should verify this URI against the current FoodOn release. 7. Tamil and Hindi local names are not established in the literature for this ingredient and are omitted; this food is not primarily a Singaporean dish requiring full Singaporean multilingual coverage — it is a Southeast Asian / Malay / Indonesian ingredient. 8. The input source (MyHealth Ministry of Health Malaysia) is not on the approved source list and has not been used as primary evidence; values have been re-anchored to FAO/INFOODS SEA and USDA FoodData Central.
